# Model Comparison Study - dataBank CI Customer 360

> *[Version française disponible : [model_comparison.md](model_comparison.md)]*

**This file is auto-generated by `ml/comparison.py`. Do not edit it by hand: changes would be overwritten at the next run.**

- Scenario A (real data only): 140 customers, 35 considered at risk (25.0%)
- Scenario B (real + synthetic data): 540 customers, 151 considered at risk (28.0%)

## Results (cross-validation: data is split into 5 groups, each group used once as the test set)

| scenario           | model                   |   auc |   recall |   precision |    f1 |
|:-------------------|:-----------------------|------:|---------:|------------:|------:|
| A (n=140 real)      | LogisticRegression     | 0.913 |    0.829 |       0.62  | 0.698 |
| A (n=140 real)      | Rule-Based Scoring     | 0.83  |    0.914 |       0.451 | 0.604 |
| B (n=540 enriched)  | LogisticRegression     | 0.944 |    0.934 |       0.737 | 0.822 |
| B (n=540 enriched)  | RandomForestClassifier | 1     |    0.987 |       1     | 0.993 |
| B (n=540 enriched)  | XGBClassifier          | 1     |    1     |       1     | 1     |

Reading note for the columns: `auc` (overall prediction quality, between 0 and 1, closer to 1 is better), `recall` (share of at-risk customers correctly spotted by the model), `precision` (share of the model's alerts that were correct), `f1` (a balanced average of recall and precision).

## How to read these results

These numbers remain indicative. See `docs/ml_problem_definition_en.md` to understand the limits of the risk indicator used and the warnings about the small sample size.

**About Scenario B's near-perfect scores (RandomForest, XGBoost):** synthetic customers are generated from real customers, through a repetition method based on business rules (see `src/synthetic_data_generator.py`). They therefore stay statistically very close to their original customer. A powerful model (random forest, boosting) can easily "memorize" these similarities. These high scores only show that the synthetic dataset is easy for these models to predict, not that they would work as well in production on new, never-seen customers.
